Ecuador 2-1 Germany
Czech Republic 0-3 Mexico
South Africa 1-0 South Korea

stories.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi set to shine in India vs Ireland 1st T20I — here's when and where to watch
stories.what: India take on Ireland in the first T20I of the 2026 series, with teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi among the headline attractions for fans. The series is generating significant buzz given Sooryavanshi's rapid rise through Indian cricket, and broadcasters have confirmed live streaming details for those eager to catch the action. It is the first match of a short T20 series between the two sides.
stories.lens: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's T20I debut or early appearances for India are a landmark moment for Indian cricket fans who have followed his meteoric rise — this is one not to miss.
stories.article:
India's blockbuster T20I series against Ireland gets underway with the first match firmly in the spotlight, thanks in large part to the anticipated appearance of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the teenager who has set Indian cricket ablaze with his batting exploits in recent months.

Sooryavanshi, who shot to national fame with his extraordinary performances at the IPL and domestic level, is expected to feature prominently and has been cited as a key attraction for viewers. The BBC reported ahead of the match that the youngster has the 'mental capabilities' to shine at international level, a ringing endorsement from observers watching his progress closely.

The Ireland series represents a calculated opportunity for India's selectors to blood young talent and test squad depth in a lower-pressure international environment ahead of bigger tournaments. Ireland, though not among the top-ranked T20 sides, have shown in recent years they are capable of springing surprises, and this tour will serve as genuine competitive preparation.

For fans, The Indian Express has confirmed live streaming options so viewers across India and beyond can follow Sooryavanshi's every move. With the series schedule and squads also confirmed by The Times of India, everything is in place for what promises to be a closely watched T20 assignment.
key_facts: India vs Ireland 1st T20I is the series opener
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is the headline attraction for India
BBC says Sooryavanshi has the 'mental capabilities' to shine
Live streaming details confirmed by The Indian Express

stories.headline: Ireland's George Dockrell excited to face 'the best' as India T20I series begins
stories.what: Ireland all-rounder George Dockrell has spoken ahead of the T20I series against India, expressing excitement at the prospect of testing himself against one of the world's top cricket sides. Dockrell, one of Ireland's most experienced international cricketers, sees the series as a major opportunity for himself and his team to measure their progress against elite opposition. Ireland have grown significantly as a T20 cricketing nation in recent years and will be looking to cause an upset.
stories.lens: Ireland's growing cricketing ambition — and their willingness to go toe-to-toe with India — is a sign of how far the sport has spread globally, and makes this series more than just a routine India win.
stories.article:
George Dockrell, Ireland's seasoned all-rounder, has set the tone for his side's T20I series against India with a declaration of intent, telling The Hindu he is genuinely excited to pit himself against 'the best' as the two countries prepare to face off.

Dockrell's comments reflect both personal ambition and a broader confidence within Irish cricket that has developed steadily over years of competing at the highest level. Having featured in landmark Irish victories in international cricket over the past decade, he brings experience and a competitive edge to what could be a challenging series against one of the game's powerhouses.

For Ireland, the series is an opportunity to demonstrate their T20 credentials on a bigger stage. They have consistently punched above their weight in global cricket, and while India — bolstered by talents like Vaibhav Sooryavanshi — will be strong favourites, the Irish will be determined not to simply make up the numbers.

Dockrell's eagerness to take on top-quality opposition speaks to the culture of ambition that has helped Ireland earn Full Member status with the ICC and qualify for major tournaments. His mindset, and that of the broader Irish squad, will be central to whether they can spring a surprise when the series gets underway.
key_facts: George Dockrell says he is excited to test himself against 'the best'
Dockrell is one of Ireland's most experienced international cricketers
Comments made ahead of Ireland vs India T20I series 2026

stories.headline: India vs Bangladesh Women's T20 World Cup: Live at Old Trafford
stories.what: India's women's cricket team faced Bangladesh in a Women's T20 World Cup match at Old Trafford, Manchester, in what was a live contest closely followed by fans across the country. The match pits India's strong batting line-up against a Bangladesh side that has shown growing competitive spirit in women's cricket. The Women's T20 World Cup, hosted in England, is a major milestone for the global women's game, with India among the tournament favourites.
stories.lens: A strong India women's T20 World Cup run would mark a significant step for Indian women's cricket and inspire millions of young female players across the country.
stories.article:
India's women's cricket team took on Bangladesh in a Women's T20 World Cup group-stage fixture at Old Trafford, Manchester, in a match that drew live commentary and widespread viewer attention in India. The encounter represents India's continued push in the tournament, with the team eager to build momentum in the group stage.

Old Trafford, one of England's most storied cricket venues, provided the backdrop for what is a must-watch game for Indian fans. Bangladesh's women's side has steadily improved in the T20 format, making them a credible, if not overwhelming, challenge for an India outfit packed with experience and firepower.

The Women's T20 World Cup is among the highest-profile events in women's cricket globally, and India's participation draws massive broadcast and streaming interest back home. A win would help consolidate India's position in the group standings ahead of the knockout stages.

For Indian cricket fans, the women's team's World Cup campaign carries significant emotional and sporting weight, especially after near-misses in previous editions. The broader cricketing community is watching to see whether this squad can go all the way.
key_facts: India vs Bangladesh at Old Trafford, Women's T20 World Cup
Match broadcast live with BBC commentary

stories.headline: Ashwin calls for more Indian players in The Hundred
stories.what: Former India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has called for more Indian cricketers to be given the opportunity to participate in The Hundred, England's flagship franchise T20 competition. Ashwin's comments come as the tournament continues to attract global talent and as the BCCI gradually relaxes restrictions on Indian players participating in overseas leagues. His voice carries considerable weight given his stature as one of India's greatest ever bowlers and his own experience of playing in various franchise leagues.
stories.lens: If the BCCI opens the door for more Indian players in The Hundred, it could significantly boost the earnings and global exposure of India's domestic cricketers, changing the landscape of how Indian talent is deployed internationally.
stories.article:
Ravichandran Ashwin has added his voice to growing calls for greater Indian representation in The Hundred, England's popular city-based franchise tournament, saying more Indian players should be allowed to participate in the competition. Ashwin's remarks reflect a wider conversation in cricket about the BCCI's policy on allowing Indian players to feature in overseas T20 leagues.

The Hundred has established itself as one of the most watched and commercially successful franchise tournaments in world cricket since its launch. While players from across the globe feature prominently, Indian cricketers — with the exception of those released by the BCCI — have largely been absent, a situation that Ashwin appears to believe should change.

Ashwin, who recently retired from international cricket after a decorated career spanning multiple formats, is now freer to speak his mind on cricketing policy matters. His comments are likely to reignite debate about whether the BCCI's restrictions on Indian players in overseas leagues remain appropriate in the current global cricket economy.

With the IPL's dominant position in the franchise cricket world secure, there is a growing argument that allowing Indian stars to participate in select overseas tournaments could benefit both the players and the global profile of Indian cricket, rather than pose any threat to the board's commercial interests.
key_facts: Ashwin calls for more Indian players to feature in The Hundred
BCCI currently restricts Indian players from most overseas T20 leagues
Ashwin recently retired from international cricket

stories.headline: Kamal Haasan Reveals Why He Left Bollywood: 'Too Many Underworld Connections'
stories.what: Veteran actor and filmmaker Kamal Haasan has candidly revealed that the primary reason he abandoned his Bollywood career was the pervasive presence of underworld connections in the Hindi film industry. Speaking openly about a chapter of his career that has long intrigued fans and film historians, Haasan indicated the criminal nexus made the environment untenable for him. The disclosure sheds fresh light on a well-known but rarely articulated problem that plagued Bollywood, particularly in earlier decades.
stories.lens: One of India's most respected actors finally names the reason behind a career pivot that shaped the course of Tamil and Indian cinema — a candid reckoning with Bollywood's dark history.
stories.article:
Kamal Haasan has spoken plainly about why he stepped back from a Bollywood career that could have taken a very different trajectory: the industry, he says, was riddled with too many underworld connections.

The actor, who has straddled Tamil, Telugu and Hindi cinema across a career spanning decades, made the revelation in a manner that underscores how widespread organised crime's influence on Bollywood once was. While the nexus between the Hindi film industry and criminal networks — particularly during the 1980s and 1990s — has been documented and discussed, it is rare for an actor of Haasan's stature to state so directly that it shaped a personal career decision.

Haasan's candour is notable given his standing as one of Indian cinema's most enduring and celebrated figures. His departure from Bollywood ultimately led him to build an extraordinary body of work in Tamil cinema, where he has been a defining presence for generations. The remarks prompt a fresh reckoning with what Indian cinema lost — and what the South gained — when he made that choice.

The disclosure resonates at a moment when Bollywood continues to grapple with questions about its culture and governance. For younger actors and industry observers, Haasan's words serve as a reminder of how structural problems in the film world once drove away top-tier talent.
key_facts: Kamal Haasan says underworld connections drove him out of Bollywood
He built his career predominantly in Tamil and Telugu cinema instead
The underworld-Bollywood nexus was a documented issue in prior decades

stories.headline: Prahaar Teaser: Rajkummar Rao Plays Ujjwal Nikam, Demands Ajmal Kasab's Hanging
stories.what: The teaser for Prahaar has been released, featuring Rajkummar Rao in the role of celebrated public prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam, who famously led the prosecution in the 26/11 Mumbai attacks case and sought the death penalty for terrorist Ajmal Kasab. The footage shows Rao embodying Nikam's courtroom intensity as he demands Kasab's hanging. The film revisits one of the most consequential legal battles in post-Independence India, bringing a pivotal chapter of contemporary history to the big screen.
stories.lens: A gripping teaser of a film centred on one of India's most high-profile terrorism prosecutions marks Rajkummar Rao's boldest real-life transformation yet — and revives memory of 26/11 for a new audience.
stories.article:
Rajkummar Rao steps into the shoes of renowned public prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam in the teaser for Prahaar, a film that dramatises the prosecution of Ajmal Kasab, the lone surviving gunman from the 26 November 2008 Mumbai terrorist attacks.

The teaser presents Rao in a commanding courtroom avatar, voicing Nikam's historic demand for Kasab's hanging — a moment that gripped the nation as the legal proceedings following the attacks unfolded. Ujjwal Nikam, Maharashtra's most high-profile special public prosecutor, was the face of the state's case against Kasab, who was eventually convicted and executed in 2012.

The choice of Rajkummar Rao for the role is significant. Known for physically and emotionally transformative performances, Rao appears to have immersed himself in Nikam's mannerisms and bearing. The film's premise puts it squarely in the tradition of Indian courtroom dramas anchored in real events, a genre that has seen renewed audience interest in recent years.

Prahaar arrives at a time when content rooted in true crime and national security events continues to draw strong viewership both in theatres and on streaming platforms. The teaser's release has already generated considerable conversation online, with audiences and critics responding to the weight of the subject matter and Rao's apparent intensity in the role.
key_facts: Rajkummar Rao plays real-life prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam in Prahaar
Film centres on the prosecution of 26/11 attacker Ajmal Kasab
Kasab demanded death penalty — Nikam led the case to conviction

stories.headline: Businessman Found Dead at Actor Krishi Thapanda's Bengaluru Home
stories.what: A businessman was found dead at the Bengaluru residence of Kannada actor Krishi Thapanda, triggering a police investigation. The circumstances surrounding the death are currently under scrutiny, with authorities examining whether foul play was involved. The incident has sent shockwaves through the Kannada film industry and raised questions about the events leading up to the discovery.
stories.lens: A death at a film star's home in Bengaluru puts the spotlight on the circumstances and brings law enforcement directly into the entertainment world.
stories.article:
A businessman was found dead at the Bengaluru home of Kannada actor Krishi Thapanda, police confirmed on Tuesday, setting off a criminal investigation into the incident.

Authorities reached the actor's residence after being alerted to the discovery. The identity of the deceased businessman has been noted by investigators, though the exact cause of death and the sequence of events leading up to it remained unclear at the time of reporting. Police are examining all angles, including the possibility of foul play.

Krishi Thapanda is a known face in the Kannada film industry, and the incident has drawn immediate attention from both media and the public. The nature of the businessman's connection to the actor and what brought him to the residence are among the central questions police are working to answer.

The case has created considerable unease within the Bengaluru entertainment community. Investigators are expected to question relevant individuals as the probe deepens. Further details are likely to emerge as the police investigation progresses.
key_facts: Businessman found dead at Kannada actor Krishi Thapanda's Bengaluru home
Police investigation launched into circumstances of death
Krishi Thapanda is a prominent figure in Kannada cinema

stories.headline: How OTT is Reimagining '90s Leading Ladies: Sonali Bendre, Madhuri Dixit, Karisma Kapoor
stories.what: A new wave of OTT productions is reviving and reframing the careers of Bollywood's biggest female stars from the 1990s — Sonali Bendre, Madhuri Dixit and Karisma Kapoor among them. Streaming platforms are offering these actresses roles with greater depth and complexity than the commercial Hindi film industry typically gave them in their peak years, allowing them to reinvent themselves for contemporary audiences. The trend reflects both the democratisation of storytelling through digital platforms and a growing appetite for women-led narratives in Indian entertainment.
stories.lens: For millions of viewers who grew up watching these stars, their OTT reinventions represent both nostalgia and a genuine reckoning with how Indian cinema has historically underused its women.
stories.article:
Three decades after they ruled the Hindi film industry, Sonali Bendre, Madhuri Dixit and Karisma Kapoor are finding new, more complex creative lives on streaming platforms — and audiences are taking notice.

OTT platforms have emerged as an unlikely second act for the biggest female stars of 1990s Bollywood. Where the commercial cinema of that era often cast these women in song-and-dance roles with limited dramatic range, streaming productions are giving them the space to inhabit layered, morally complex characters. Madhuri Dixit's work on Netflix's Finding Anamika, Karisma Kapoor's turn in Mentalhood, and Sonali Bendre's appearances in digital projects have all been cited as evidence of this transformation.

The phenomenon speaks to a structural shift in Indian entertainment. OTT platforms, competing fiercely for subscribers, have an incentive to develop content that appeals across age groups — and that includes viewers who grew up idolising these stars and are now eager to see them in stories that reflect adult realities. The stars themselves have spoken about valuing creative freedom over commercial formulas.

For film historians and gender critics, the trend also raises a pointed question: why did it take streaming to unlock the full potential of actresses who were always capable of more? The answer, many suggest, lies in the patriarchal structures of mainstream Bollywood that OTT, for all its imperfections, is quietly beginning to dismantle.
key_facts: Sonali Bendre, Madhuri Dixit and Karisma Kapoor finding renewed careers on OTT
Streaming platforms offering more complex roles than 1990s Bollywood did
Trend reflects growing appetite for women-led narratives in Indian streaming

stories.headline: Soni Razdan Defends Alia Bhatt: 'Not Her Fault She's Daughter of Industry Insiders'
stories.what: Actor Soni Razdan has come out in defence of her daughter Alia Bhatt, pushing back against the nepotism narrative that has long shadowed the actress's career. Razdan argued that it is not Alia's fault that she was born into a family with deep connections to the Hindi film industry, and that the actress should not be penalised for circumstances of birth she had no control over. The remarks reignite a debate that has been central to Bollywood's public discourse for years, particularly since the death of Sushant Singh Rajput brought questions of industry access and privilege to a national boil.
stories.lens: The nepotism debate in Bollywood has shaped public perception of several major stars — Soni Razdan's defence of Alia Bhatt keeps that conversation alive and asks a genuinely difficult question about fairness and talent.
stories.article:
Soni Razdan has addressed one of Bollywood's most persistent controversies head-on, arguing that her daughter Alia Bhatt cannot fairly be blamed for being born into a family of film industry insiders.

Razdan's remarks push back against the nepotism discourse that has trailed Alia Bhatt since the actress's debut. The argument is straightforward: no one chooses their parents or the advantages those parents confer, and penalising someone for a circumstance of birth is itself a form of unfairness. Razdan is a respected actress in her own right, and her willingness to speak publicly on the subject adds a maternal as well as an industry-insider perspective to a debate that often lacks nuance.

Alia Bhatt has, in recent years, built a body of work — including critically lauded performances in films like Gangubai Kathiawadi and Raazi — that many argue stands entirely on its own merits. Supporters contend that whatever door her surname opened, she has delivered the goods once inside. Critics, however, maintain that the structural advantages of a famous family background mean that equally talented actors from outside the industry never get the same door to open.

The nepotism debate in Bollywood shows little sign of fading. Soni Razdan's comments are unlikely to settle the argument, but they do add a dimension often missing from public discussion: the perspective of those inside the equation who believe the framing of inherited privilege misses the complexity of individual achievement.
key_facts: Soni Razdan says it's not Alia Bhatt's fault she was born to industry insiders
Alia Bhatt has been a frequent target of Bollywood nepotism criticism
Razdan is herself a veteran Bollywood actress
